topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring little chime in the quiet hum of my afternoon. My breath hitched, a sudden tightness in my chest that made it hard to draw a full breath. It was a request. From Mark. Mark, who used to make my stomach clench just thinking about his shadow looming over me in the hallway. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally tapped it. A wave of nausea washed over me, cold and unwelcome. I stared at the profile picture—a smug grin I remembered too well—and felt the familiar tightening around my throat. Why now? What did he want? I quickly minimized the message, pulling my hoodie tighter around myself, wishing the floor would just swallow me whole.

[story 2]

I found myself staring at my phone for what felt like an hour, the screen illuminating the worried crease between my eyebrows. When the notification finally appeared, it was his name. A jolt went through me, sharp and unwelcome, like static electricity. My hands started to shake, and I had to grip the edge of the desk to steady them. Opening the chat felt like stepping onto thin ice. Every word he might send was a potential trigger, a memory resurfacing with unwelcome clarity. I reread the request three times, my heart thumping a frantic rhythm against my ribs. I closed the app abruptly, leaning back in my chair, trying to regulate the rapid flutter in my chest, focusing instead on the pattern of the wood grain on my desk.

[story 3]

The sound of the phone alerted me, and I flinched, dropping it onto the rug. It was Mark. My vision blurred for a moment as I looked at the screen, the text glowing starkly in the dim light. A profound sense of dread settled deep in my core, making my limbs feel heavy and unresponsive. I didn't know what to do with it. Should I ignore it? Should I block him immediately? My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles strain in my neck. I took a slow, deliberate inhale, trying to force the frantic energy draining from my system to settle, hoping the silence on the other end would be preferable to whatever this intrusion represented.
